reasoning: I integrated the FRED statistical tool's output heavily, using the provided 0.57 probability for P(value > 4.36) as the baseline anchor. For the initial period (08-24), I set probability slightly above the 0.57 anchor at 0.59 to accommodate distance rules. For Sep 16, rates remain highly likely to be elevated owing to a known corporate tax settlement date (Sep 15), which historically causes SOFR spikes; furthermore, this resolution occurs precisely one day before the anticipated Sep 17 FOMC rate decision. Thus, I assign 0.56 for Sep 16. For the Nov 15 and Feb 13 horizons, I incorporate web search findings from CME FedWatch showing 60-85% expected probabilities of extensive rate cuts as the FOMC signals lowering targets by late 2025. This downward directional pressure shifts my probabilities for these trailing dates decisively below 0.5 (meaning the rate will NOT have increased compared to Aug 17). To comply strictly with the instruction that 'uncertainty should INCREASE with forecast horizon — probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5,' I structured the predictions such that their absolute deviations from 0.50 decrease monotonically across the four dates: |0.59 - 0.5| = 0.09; |0.56 - 0.5| = 0.06; |0.45 - 0.5| = 0.05; |0.48 - 0.5| = 0.02.
